subject: Mathematics Extension 1
sourceUrl: NESA 2019 HSC Mathematics Extension 1 — Notes from the Marking Centre (local PDF)
generalFeedback: use the mark value of a question as a guide to the complexity of solution required.
reading the question carefully in order to answer the question that is asked.
reading the question accurately to determine the relationships between variables
interpreting the wording of a question
fulfilling the requirements of the question
understand the interrelatedness of the parts of a question
developing an understanding of how the various parts of a question are related
take time to plan their approach to a proof, considering the facts needed and how to most easily obtain them.
using the Reference Sheet to help check their integration of standard expressions
balancing time throughout the exam to allow more time to focus on difficult last questions.

betterResponses: gradients of the given lines and used the formula 𝜃 = tan−1 |𝑚1− 𝑚2 / 1+𝑚1 𝑚2| correctly to find the acute angle between the lines
angle that each line makes with the horizontal and, by subtraction, found the correct angle between the lines.
areasToImprove: making the variable 𝑦 the subject so that the gradient can be read off from the equation
ensuring the formula 𝜃 = tan−1 |𝑚1− 𝑚2 / 1+𝑚1 𝑚2| is correctly written and used with particular attention to the signs in the numerator and denominator
understanding the purpose and correct use of the absolute value
understanding finding the point of intersection of the two lines has no bearing on the answer.

betterResponses: use the method of multiplying by the square of the denominator
find the critical points and tested points on either side to reach the correct solution
consider options to satisfy the given condition: 𝑥 + 1 > 0 or 𝑥 + 1 < 0
use the graph of the hyperbola and the straight line 𝑦 = 2 and the point of intersection to solve the inequality.
areasToImprove: understanding the sign implications if there is a variable in the denominator of an inequality
working on algebraic skills when attempting to multiply by the square of the denominator
practising solving quadratic inequalities
avoiding writing that −1 < 𝑥 < −2 on the number line when testing points or solving the quadratic inequality.

betterResponses: present a proof that used appropriate theorems supported by reasons that were clear and concise
provide proofs that were well structured and easy to follow
use conventional symbols and abbreviations correctly.
areasToImprove: exercising caution in labelling angles correctly
supporting each claim in a proof with a valid reason that is expressed clearly and concisely
developing the proof in a logical manner
distinguishing between similar and congruent triangles.

betterResponses: execute the long division of the polynomial to correctly find the quotient 𝑄(𝑥)
establish that the quotient 𝑄(𝑥) is a quadratic and then correctly found the coefficients of this quadratic either by equating coefficients or by inspection.
areasToImprove: understanding the remainder theorem and how to apply it
accurately performing long division of a polynomial
recognising that in this question the quotient 𝑄(𝑥) needs to be a quadratic.

betterResponses: start with the double angle formula and logically worked towards the reduced form that needed to be integrated
perform the integration with the proper treatment of the constants involved in the integrand.
areasToImprove: recognising the need to reduce the given trigonometric function so that it can be integrated
using a method to express sin2 4𝑥 in terms of cos 8𝑥 rather than relying on memorisation of a formula
using the Reference Sheet to help check their integration of standard expressions
paying attention to the constants associated with standard integrals.

betterResponses: recognise that binomial probability modelling was needed and used the appropriate values in the relevant binomial term.
areasToImprove: recognising when a problem involves a binomial probability term
determining the correct values for 𝑝, 𝑞 and 𝑛.

betterResponses: make effective use of the complementary event to determine the probability.
areasToImprove: understanding what ‘at least’ means in the context of probability problems
clearly articulating that 𝑃(at least 2 wins) = 1 − 𝑃(0 wins) − 𝑃(1 win)
understanding that it is more efficient to present the probability using sigma notation rather than writing out all the terms in expanded form
paying particular attention to use the correct values for 𝑝, 𝑞 and 𝑛, and for the coefficient (𝑛/𝑟).

betterResponses: show the use of the chain rule
show their result for 𝑑𝐵/𝑑𝐴
evaluate 𝐵 correctly and substituted it correctly into the chain rule.
areasToImprove: using the calculator correctly
differentiating to find 𝑑𝐵/𝑑𝐴.

betterResponses: find the values for 𝑅 and 𝛼 (in exact form) efficiently.
areasToImprove: applying the auxilliary angle/transformation method skillfully
knowing and using exact radian values.

betterResponses: realise that the particle would be at rest at the end points of its motion and that this equated to the amplitude of their expression from part (i)
areasToImprove: understanding the features of Simple Harmonic Motion
reading the question carefully in order to answer the question that is asked.

betterResponses: realise that maximum velocity would be the amplitude of their expression for velocity
understand that speed would be a positive value
deduce that a 4th quadrant result to their trigonometric equation would still yield a positive value for time.
areasToImprove: understanding the difference between speed and velocity
developing their ability to solve trigonometric equations and working with exact trigonometric values.

betterResponses: use the definition of a parabola to determine that 𝑃𝑆 = 𝑃𝑀
find the length of 𝑃𝑀 using the fact that it was a vertical line and avoided using the distance formula
use the formula for the equation of the tangent from the Reference Sheet to find the coordinates of 𝑅
determine the length of 𝑆𝑅 without the use of the distance formula
show that 𝑆𝑅 = 𝑃𝑆 and used this to clearly justify why ∠𝑆𝑃𝑅 = ∠𝑆𝑅𝑃
use other methods such as the reflective property of the parabola to achieve the required result.
areasToImprove: enhancing algebraic skills to avoid complicating algebraic manipulations.

betterResponses: to quickly and efficiently obtain this result.
areasToImprove: knowing the method and the requirements of the question.

betterResponses: connect the right times and temperatures and work carefully through finding 𝐴, 𝑘 and then 𝑑𝑇/𝑑𝑡
use exact values or calculator memories to maintain accuracy as they move through the stages of finding 𝐴, 𝑘 and then 𝑑𝑇/𝑑𝑡.
areasToImprove: reading the question accurately to determine the relationships between variables
using the calculator accurately
showing all working out.

betterResponses: differentiate 𝑢 = cos2 𝑥 correctly
substitute correctly for 𝑢
change the bounding limits and place them in the correct positions
integrate and substitute correctly to arrive at the solution.
areasToImprove: avoiding common errors such as
providing the correct integrand,
using the original limits
providing the correct integrand but placing the limits incorrectly
differentiating incorrectly
making errors with negative signs
avoiding careless substitution errors.

betterResponses: equate the coefficients in terms of 𝑘 and 𝑘 + 1, expand and solve the resulting equation
manipulate the expansion of the binomial coefficient and the indices successfully
use guess, check and refine to arrive at the solution, provide the substitution line and supporting evidence.
areasToImprove: using brackets carefully when writing out an expansion
correctly recalling and using of the formula for (𝑛/𝑘)
accurately applying algebraic skills when manipulating indices.

betterResponses: manipulate the fractional indices and integrate successfully
include and find the constant of integration
show specific use of given conditions to order to arrive at the correct positive solution.
areasToImprove: practising integration involving exponents with fractional indices
ensuring the inclusion of the constant of integration
including all reasoning in a ‘show’ question.

betterResponses: use the reciprocal of 𝑣 = 𝑑𝑥/𝑑𝑡 to integrate to find an expression for 𝑡, use the given conditions to find the constant of integration
use separation of variables and then integrated to find an expression for 𝑡 and use the given conditions to find the constant of integration
manipulate exponential equations into logarithmic format in order to provide an expression of 𝑥 as a function of 𝑡.
areasToImprove: fulfilling the requirements of the question
providing an expression of 𝑥 as a function of 𝑡
practising the method of finding the reciprocal of 𝑣 = 𝑑𝑥/𝑑𝑡 to integrate to find an expression for 𝑡
remembering to include the constant of integration.

betterResponses: solve for 𝑡 and hence 𝑥, and use either Pythagoras’ theorem or trigonometry to find the required distance
find the Cartesian equation of motion and solved for 𝑥, and used either Pythagoras’ theorem or trigonometry to find the required distance.
areasToImprove: interpreting the requirements of the question correctly in order to find either 𝑥 or 𝑡 according to the given conditions.
working carefully through multiple steps to arrive at the solution without making errors.

betterResponses: substitute the correct value of 𝑥 from part (i) into the Cartesian equation to get the gradient of the tangent at 𝐴
use the respective gradients in the formula tan 𝜃 = [𝑚1−𝑚2 / 1−𝑚1 ×𝑚2] to arrive at the angle
use the components of velocity to solve tan 𝛼 = [𝑦̇/𝑥̇] to obtain the angle of impact and then calculated the angle with the plane by appropriate subtraction.
areasToImprove: avoiding errors when substituting values into the Cartesian equation, when substituting into tan 𝛼 = [𝑦̇/𝑥̇], or when substituting into tan 𝜃 = [𝑚1−𝑚2 / 1−𝑚1 ×𝑚2].

betterResponses: clearly show the inductive step using the correct assumption in the 𝑛 = 𝑘 + 1 case
show their knowledge of algebra using factorial terms
display sufficient working to indicate a correct proof and obtain full marks.
areasToImprove: understanding how to manipulate expressions that involve factorials,
recognising that (𝑘 + 1)! = (𝑘 + 1)𝑘! .

betterResponses: equate functions to find points of intersection
understand the connection between the points of intersection of two graphs, the roots of an equation and the zeros of a function.
areasToImprove: interpreting the wording of a question
understanding and obeying the instruction ‘By considering graphs, explain…’.
giving sufficient information in explanations.

betterResponses: use the Reference Sheet and Newton’s method to fully show the required result.
areasToImprove: developing accurate and efficient differentiation and algebraic skills.

betterResponses: realise that showing that the function values 𝑓(𝑥1 ) < 0 and 𝑓(𝑥2 ) > 0 were integral to achieving the required result
use algebra and inequality arguments to show that 𝑓(𝑥2 ) > 0
use a graphical approach arguing their solution from the shape of the cubic polynomial and the position of the stationary points and zeroes.
areasToImprove: knowing how to manipulate inequalities
solving, expanding and factorising algebraic expressions involving multiple terms and cubics.

betterResponses: use the connection between common tangents, gradients and equal derivative values.
areasToImprove: understanding the difference between a function and a function value.

betterResponses: use an appropriate identity or concavity argument to show the required result
understand the boundary conditions on the variables 𝑘 and 𝛼 and the connection between 𝑥0 and 𝑥0 − 𝛼.
areasToImprove: understanding the evenness or oddness of sine and cosine functions
knowing the general results of trigonometric equations.

betterResponses: use part (ii) to forge a correct expression for 𝑘
interpret the question to find 𝑘 in terms of 𝛼 only and realise that 𝑥0 = 𝛼 − 𝑥0 .
areasToImprove: developing an understanding of how the various parts of a question are related
balancing time throughout the exam to allow more time to focus on difficult last questions.
